title: holodiscus argenteus var. velutinus
intro: Holodiscus argenteus var. velutinus, also known as velvety mountain ash, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It belongs to the Rosaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions, due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Velvety mountain ash can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
